commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8ea5dbb28b3ea8c4fe3414cb1f787d6328270abd)
commands.review_summary:
The PR threads gateway event-loop health into channel health monitoring so unhealthy channel restarts are deferred while the event loop is degraded.

PR surface: Source +38, Tests +39. Total +77 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows a reload plan can restart the health monitor, and the replacement callback is separate from the startup call changed by the PR. The linked production Telegram crash-loop still lacks a deterministic live reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The code repair is narrow, but insufficient structured real-behavior proof makes this a human/contributor-action merge blocker rather than a repair-lane candidate.

target.title: fix(gateway): defer channel health restarts while the event loop is saturated
target.body:
Fixes #89785

### What & why

Under Telegram multi-account churn, the gateway can enter a fixed-interval 502 crash-loop. The channel health monitor restart-cascades against a channel start/connect phase that is *slow because the whole event loop is saturated* — not because the channel is dead. Each forced restart re-enters the same slow start and adds more startup work to an already-pegged loop, so the next cycle sees the same "unhealthy" channel and restarts again. In the report this was ~7–10 restarts over ~64 minutes, each preceded by a liveness warning at `channels.telegram.start-account` with `eventLoopDelayMaxMs ≈ 21000`, `eventLoopUtilization ≈ 0.97`. It self-resolved once the churn settled.

### Root cause

`startChannelHealthMonitor` (`src/gateway/channel-health-monitor.ts`) restarts any account the policy flags as unhealthy. During event-loop saturation a still-connecting channel is past its connect grace and reports `connected: false`, so the policy returns `disconnected` and the monitor restarts it. Restarting during saturation is counterproductive and is what turns a transient slow start into a sustained cascade.

### The fix

The gateway already samples its own event-loop health (`createGatewayEventLoopHealthMonitor`, exposed as `readinessEventLoopHealth.snapshot`). This PR threads that signal into the channel health monitor and **defers channel restarts on any cycle where the event loop is `degraded`**, logging the deferral with the saturation reasons.

- No change to the health *policy* — channels are still classified the same way.
- When the loop is healthy, restarts behave exactly as before (dead channels are still recovered).
- A genuinely dead channel under sustained saturation is simply restarted on a later cycle, once the loop recovers — which is the behavior the issue asks for ("a slow channel bind … should be allowed to complete (or fail cleanly) without the gateway being declared unhealthy and force-restarted into the same stall").

The new dependency is opt-in (`getEventLoopHealth?`), so existing callers and tests are unaffected. Files: `channel-health-monitor.ts` (+ wiring in `server-runtime-startup-services.ts` and `server.impl.ts`) and the monitor test. +77 lines, additive only.

### Real behavior proof

**Setup.** A small harness runs the **real** channel health monitor and the **real** gateway event-loop health monitor (`node:perf_hooks`) under **real** event-loop saturation — a synchronous busy-block (~1.2s of every ~1.4s) that mirrors the multi-account CPU pressure from the report. The monitored account is a Telegram account stuck in start/connect (`running: true`, `connected: false`, started past the connect grace). Time is compressed (1s check interval) so the ~64-minute production cascade shows in ~15s; the decision logic and the saturation signal are the production ones.

The A/B is exactly the shape of the fix (the new signal is opt-in):
- **before** = monitor started without `getEventLoopHealth` (shipped behavior)
- **after**  = monitor started with `getEventLoopHealth` (this PR's wiring)

**Command.**
```
node --import tsx repro_89785.ts before
node --import tsx repro_89785.ts after
```

**Observed result.**

`before` — restart-cascade reproduced (real event-loop delay ~1220 ms, utilization ~0.86 → `degraded=true`), capped only by the restarts/hour limit, mirroring the report's ~10 restarts:
```
[health-monitor] [telegram:acct1] health-monitor: restarting (reason: disconnected)
  RESTART #1  ... WHILE event-loop delayMax=20.3ms   util=0.041 degraded=false
  RESTART #2  ... WHILE event-loop delayMax=1211.1ms util=0.754 degraded=true
  ...
  RESTART #10 ... WHILE event-loop delayMax=1219.5ms util=0.86  degraded=true
[health-monitor] [telegram:acct1] health-monitor: hit 10 restarts/hour limit, skipping
RESULT [before]: 10 forced restart(s) under sustained event-loop saturation
```

`after` — one restart while the loop was still healthy (correct: a real disconnect is still recovered), then deferred for every saturated cycle:
```
  [health-check] event-loop: delayMax=20.2ms   degraded=false -> allow restarts
  RESTART #1 ... WHILE event-loop delayMax=20.2ms degraded=false
  [health-check] event-loop: delayMax=1219.5ms degraded=true reasons=[event_loop_delay] -> DEFER restarts
[health-monitor] deferring channel restarts while gateway event loop is degraded (reasons: event_loop_delay)
  ... (deferred every subsequent saturated cycle) ...
RESULT [after]: 1 forced restart(s) under sustained event-loop saturation
```

So: **before = 10 forced restarts (cascade); after = 1 (only while healthy), then deferred.**

**Automated checks.**
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/channel-health-monitor.test.ts src/gateway/channel-health-policy.test.ts` → all pass (incl. 2 new monitor tests covering defer-while-degraded and resume-on-recovery).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-runtime-services.test.ts` → pass (wiring).
- `pnpm tsgo:core` and `pnpm tsgo:core:test` → clean.

**Not tested.** I did not reproduce the full live Telegram multi-account crash-loop end-to-end (the report notes it was not deterministically reproducible); the harness uses a stubbed channel, compressed time, and synthetic event-loop saturation rather than real churn. The harness itself is not part of this PR.

### Notes

- AI-assisted (developed with Claude Code). I understand and have reviewed the change.
- No `CHANGELOG.md` edits.

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
